ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employment_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "indicator_code" TEXT /* Code of the employment indicator */,
  "indicator_description" TEXT /* Description of the employment indicator */,
  "branch_code" TEXT /* Code of the industry branch */,
  "industry_breakdown" TEXT /* Detailed breakdown of the industry */,
  "sector_code" TEXT /* Code of the economic sector */,
  "institutional_sector" TEXT /* Institutional sector (e.g., Total economy) */,
  "frequency_code" TEXT /* Code of the data frequency */,
  "time_period" INTEGER /* Time period of the data */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the employment statistic (in thousands of persons) */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Code of the geographic region */,
  "sub_sector_code" TEXT /* Code of the sub-sector within the economic sector */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "is_estimate" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the value is an estimate */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the data entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_indicator_code FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_code") REFERENCES indicators ("indicator_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_frequency_code FOREIGN KEY ("frequency_code") REFERENCES frequencies ("frequency_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_time_period FOREIGN KEY ("time_period") REFERENCES time_periods ("time_period_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_institutional_sector FOREIGN KEY ("institutional_sector") REFERENCES institutional_sectors ("institutional_sector"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_branch_code FOREIGN KEY ("branch_code") REFERENCES industry_branches ("branch_code"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_data_sector_code FOREIGN KEY ("sector_code") REFERENCES economic_sectors ("sector_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_code" TEXT /* Unique code of the employment indicator */,
  "indicator_description" TEXT /* Description of the employment indicator */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "industry_branches" (
  "branch_code" TEXT /* Unique code of the industry branch */,
  "branch_description" TEXT /* Description of the industry branch */,
  "parent_branch_code" TEXT /* Code of the parent industry branch */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("branch_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_sectors" (
  "sector_code" TEXT /* Unique code of the economic sector */,
  "sector_description" TEXT /* Description of the economic sector */,
  "parent_sector_code" TEXT /* Code of the parent economic sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sector_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutional_sectors" (
  "institutional_sector" TEXT /* Unique code of the institutional sector */,
  "sector_description" TEXT /* Description of the institutional sector */,
  "parent_institutional_sector" TEXT /* Code of the parent institutional sector */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("institutional_sector")
)

CREATE TABLE "frequencies" (
  "frequency_code" TEXT /* Unique code of the data frequency */,
  "frequency_description" TEXT /* Description of the data frequency */,
  "interval_days" INTEGER /* Number of days in each frequency interval */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("frequency_code")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_periods" (
  "time_period_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each time period */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the time period */,
  "quarter" INTEGER /* Quarter of the time period */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the time period */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the time period */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the time period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("time_period_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, researcher, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department or team the user belongs to */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login date of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the data was accessed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES employment_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data entry being versioned */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Description of changes made in this version */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "approved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who approved the version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_approved_by FOREIGN KEY ("approved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES employment_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
